introSection: Dynamite is a steel roller coaster located at Freizeitpark Plohn in Lengenfeld, Saxony, Germany.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In January 2017, Freizeitpark Plohn revealed its plans to retire the 45-year-old Silver Mine roller coaster and install a replacement attraction which would open in 2019. In April of the same year, further details were revealed including the type of roller coaster, at the time simply referred to as a BigDipper roller coaster.

The name was revealed in August 2017 along with some additional details: the ride would be over tall and reach a maximum speed of.

Silver Mine was dismantled in November 2017 with groundwork beginning shortly after.

On 12 August 2018, the park revealed Dynamite's layout.

Dynamite opened on 18 May 2019.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
Upon its opening, Dynamite was the single largest investment made by the park.

Dynamite is the second BigDipper installation from Mack Rides, the first being Lost Gravity which opened at Walibi Holland in 2016. It has a tunnel and a dive drop, similar to that of a Bolliger & Mabillard Wing Coaster. There are two more inversions; a vertical loop and a zero-g roll.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The two outer seats on each row are floorless.

fullText:
Dynamite is a steel roller coaster located at Freizeitpark Plohn in Lengenfeld, Saxony, Germany.

In January 2017, Freizeitpark Plohn revealed its plans to retire the 45-year-old Silver Mine roller coaster and install a replacement attraction which would open in 2019. In April of the same year, further details were revealed including the type of roller coaster, at the time simply referred to as a BigDipper roller coaster.

The name was revealed in August 2017 along with some additional details: the ride would be over tall and reach a maximum speed of.

Silver Mine was dismantled in November 2017 with groundwork beginning shortly after.

On 12 August 2018, the park revealed Dynamite's layout.

Dynamite opened on 18 May 2019.

Upon its opening, Dynamite was the single largest investment made by the park.

Dynamite is the second BigDipper installation from Mack Rides, the first being Lost Gravity which opened at Walibi Holland in 2016. It has a tunnel and a dive drop, similar to that of a Bolliger & Mabillard Wing Coaster. There are two more inversions; a vertical loop and a zero-g roll.

The two outer seats on each row are floorless.
